Title: Tartary Buckwheat R2R3-MYB Gene FtMYB3 Negatively Regulates Anthocyanin and Proanthocyanin Biosynthesis

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: FtMYB3 promoter contains multiple cis-elements responsive to light, low temperature, drought and hormones (GA, auxin, SA, MeJA). The gene is strongly induced by MeJA and has been previously shown to interact with FtJAZ2, suggesting integration with jasmonate signaling in regulating anthocyanin/PA biosynthesis.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. FtMYB3 encodes a subgroup 4 R2R3-MYB transcription factor with conserved R2/R3 domains, bHLH interaction motif and C-terminal C1/C2 repression motifs.
2. FtMYB3 is predominantly expressed in roots and is weakly expressed in flowers and seeds; its expression is induced by SA, MeJA, drought (PEG6000), salt (NaCl) and low temperature.
3. Ectopic overexpression of FtMYB3 in tobacco and Arabidopsis reduces anthocyanin and PA contents, leading to lighter floral and seed coat pigmentation, while increasing rutin levels in Arabidopsis.
4. In Arabidopsis overexpressing FtMYB3, expression of late flavonoid biosynthetic genes DFR, ANS and BAN and the PA transporter gene TT13 is significantly downregulated, whereas early biosynthetic genes are largely unaffected and FLS can be upregulated.
5. FtMYB3 physically interacts with bHLH and WD40 proteins (TT8, GL3, EGL3, TTG1) from Tartary buckwheat and Arabidopsis, consistent with a role in MBW complexes.
6. Transient expression assays show that FtMYB3 represses the promoter activity of both AtDFR and FtDFR2, confirming direct transcriptional repression of DFR genes.
7. Overall, FtMYB3 acts as an R2R3-MYB repressor that negatively regulates anthocyanin and PA biosynthesis in Tartary buckwheat and in heterologous systems.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: Tartary buckwheat genomic database and an internal transcriptomic dataset (data not shown) were used to identify FtMYB3.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a transcriptional repressor that decreases anthocyanin and proanthocyanidin accumulation, leading to reduced pigmentation in tobacco petals and Arabidopsis seedlings and seed coats.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Mechanism: SG4-type R2R3-MYB repressor that (1) forms complexes with bHLH and WD40 proteins (TT8/GL3/EGL3/TTG1) to inhibit transcriptional activation of late flavonoid biosynthetic and PA transport genes, and (2) directly represses promoter activity of DFR genes (AtDFR and FtDFR2).
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: Functions within the MYB-bHLH-WD40 (MBW) regulatory network controlling anthocyanin and proanthocyanidin biosynthesis, acting downstream of SA and MeJA and abiotic stress signals.
Plant_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Flavonoid biosynthesis pathway, specifically the anthocyanin and proanthocyanidin branches (late biosynthetic steps involving DFR, ANS, BAN and PA transport via TT13)
Plant_Genes.Upstream_or_Downstream_Regulation: FtMYB3 --| (transcriptional repression in Arabidopsis overexpression lines) --> AtDFR; FtMYB3 --| --> AtANS; FtMYB3 --| --> AtBAN; FtMYB3 --| --> AtTT13 (PA transporter gene); FtMYB3 --| (direct promoter repression in transient assays) --> P_AtDFR; FtMYB3 --| --> P_FtDFR2.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB transcription factor, subgroup 4 (SG4), with N-terminal R2 and R3 DNA-binding domains, an R3 bHLH-interaction motif and C-terminal C1/C2 repression motifs
Plant_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: FtTT8, FtGL3, FtEGL3, FtTTG1 (Tartary buckwheat bHLH and WD40 proteins); AtGL3, AtEGL3, AtTTG1 and TT8 (Arabidopsis orthologs), interacting with FtMYB3 in yeast two-hybrid assays.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Highly expressed in roots, moderate in stems and leaves, very low in flowers and seeds at flowering stage; transcription strongly induced in seedlings by SA, MeJA, PEG6000 (drought), NaCl (salt) and low temperature.
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Hormone treatments: salicylic acid (1 mmol/L SA) and methyl jasmonate (2 mmol/L MeJA); abiotic stresses: 30% PEG6000 (drought), 150 mmol/L NaCl (salt), low temperature; time course 0–15 h.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Regulation of anthocyanin and proanthocyanidin biosynthesis and stress-responsive flavonoid metabolism in Tartary buckwheat
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Anthocyanin and proanthocyanidin accumulation (flavonoid content); abiotic stress response (salt, drought, cold); secondary metabolism
Plant_Genes.Quantitative_Phenotypic_Alterations: In transgenic tobacco flowers overexpressing FtMYB3, total anthocyanin content decreased by 38.75% and 21.25% relative to WT. In Arabidopsis overexpressing FtMYB3, rutin content increased to 109.8% and 119.3% of WT, while anthocyanin and PA contents were significantly reduced (p < 0.01).
Plant_Genes.Other_Phenotypic_Effects: Ectopic expression in Arabidopsis increases rutin (flavonol) content while decreasing anthocyanin and PA levels.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Tartary buckwheat cultivar Xiqiao No. 2; Arabidopsis thaliana ecotype Col-0; Nicotiana tabacum cultivar NC89; stable transgenic Arabidopsis (At #1, At #2) and tobacco (Nt #1, Nt #2) lines overexpressing FtMYB3.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: qRT-PCR for spatial and stress-induced expression; promoter cis-element analysis; stable transformation of Arabidopsis and tobacco with pCHF3-FtMYB3-eYFP; measurement of anthocyanin, PA and rutin contents (spectrophotometry and HPLC); qRT-PCR of flavonoid pathway genes; yeast two-hybrid assays; transient tobacco leaf assays with DFR promoter–GUS constructs.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Functional validation by stable overexpression in Arabidopsis and tobacco with metabolite quantification (anthocyanin, PA, rutin), qRT-PCR analysis of flavonoid structural genes (EBGs and LBGs), yeast two-hybrid interaction assays with bHLH/WD40 partners, and transient repression of AtDFR and FtDFR2 promoters driving GUS.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified from Tartary buckwheat genomic and in-house transcriptomic databases and from the published FtMYB3 sequence (GenBank JF313349); full-length ORF cloned by PCR from cDNA of Xiqiao No. 2.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: FtMYB3 is a negative regulator of anthocyanin and PA accumulation and is proposed as a potential target gene for improving Tartary buckwheat quality by modulating flavonoid composition and pigment levels, for example via gene editing or downregulation to enhance anthocyanin/PA contents and associated antioxidant capacity.
